title: ConstructionAxisInput Object
description: A ConstructionAxisInput is a throwaway object used to create a ConstructionAxis The usage pattern is: a. create a ConstructionAxisInput (ConstructionAxes.CreateInput) b. call one of the member functions to specify how the ConstructionAxis is created c. create the ConstructionAxis (call ConstructionAxes.Add) d. stop referencing the ConstructionAxisInput (so it gets deleted).

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: setByCircularFace
methods.description: This input method is for creating an axis coincident with the axis of a cylindrical, conical or torus face.   This can result in a parametric or non-parametric construction axis depending on whether the parent component is parametric or is a direct edit component.

methods.name: setByEdge
methods.description: This input method is for creating a construction axis from a specified linear/circular edge or sketch curve. This can result in a parametric or non-parametric construction axis depending on whether the parent component is parametric or is a direct edit component.

methods.name: setByLine
methods.description: This input method is for creating a non-parametric construction axis whose position in space is defined by an InfiniteLine3D object.   This method of defining a construction axis is only valid when working in the direct modeling mode. This is not valid when working in the parametric modeling mode and will fail.

methods.name: setByNormalToFaceAtPoint
methods.description: This input method if for creating a construction axis normal to a specified face or sketch profile and that passes through a specified point. This can result in a parametric or non-parametric construction axis depending on whether the parent component is parametric or is a direct edit component.

methods.name: setByPerpendicularAtPoint
methods.description: This input method is for creating an axis that is normal to a face at a specified point.

methods.name: setByTwoPlanes
methods.description: This input method is for creating a construction axis coincident with the intersection of two planes or planar faces. This will fail if the two planes are parallel. This can result in a parametric or non-parametric construction axis depending on whether the parent component is parametric or is a direct edit component.

methods.name: setByTwoPoints
methods.description: This input method is for creating a construction axis that passes through the two points (work points, sketch points or vertices). This will fail if the two points are coincident. This can result in a parametric or non-parametric construction axis depending on whether the parent component is parametric or is a direct edit component.

properties.name: creationOccurrence
properties.description: In order for geometry to be transformed correctly, an Occurrence for creation needs to be specified when the ConstructionAxis is created based on geometry (e.g. a straight edge) in another component AND (the ConstructionAxis) is not in the root component. The CreationOccurrence is analogous to the active occurrence in the UI

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: targetBaseOrFormFeature
properties.description: When creating a construction axis that is owned by a base or form feature, set this property to the base or form feature you want to associate the new construction plane with. By default, this is null, meaning it will not be associated with a base or form feature.   Because of a current limitation, if you want to create a construction axis associated with a base or form feature, you must set this property AND call the startEdit method of the base or form feature, create the feature, and then call the finishEdit method of the base or form feature. The base or form feature must be in an "edit" state to be able to add any additional items to it.

related_samples.name: Construction Axis API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating construction axis in various ways.

classType.title: ConstructionAxisInput.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

setByCircularFace.title: ConstructionAxisInput.setByCircularFace Method
setByCircularFace.description: This input method is for creating an axis coincident with the axis of a cylindrical, conical or torus face. This can result in a parametric or non-parametric construction axis depending on whether the parent component is parametric or is a direct edit component.
setByCircularFace.return_description: Returns true if the creation of the ConstructionAxisInput is successful.

parameters.name: circularFace
parameters.description: The face from a cylinder, cone, or torus.

related_samples.name: Construction Axis API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating construction axis in various ways.

setByEdge.title: ConstructionAxisInput.setByEdge Method
setByEdge.description: This input method is for creating a construction axis from a specified linear/circular edge or sketch curve. This can result in a parametric or non-parametric construction axis depending on whether the parent component is parametric or is a direct edit component.
setByEdge.return_description: Returns true if the creation of the ConstructionAxisInput is successful.

parameters.name: edgeEntity
parameters.description: A linear/circular edge, construction line, or sketch line

related_samples.name: Construction Axis API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating construction axis in various ways.

setByLine.title: ConstructionAxisInput.setByLine Method
setByLine.description: This input method is for creating a non-parametric construction axis whose position in space is defined by an InfiniteLine3D object. This method of defining a construction axis is only valid when working in the direct modeling mode. This is not valid when working in the parametric modeling mode and will fail.
setByLine.return_description: Returns true if the creation of the ConstructionAxisInput is successful.

related_samples.name: Construction Axis API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating construction axis in various ways.

setByNormalToFaceAtPoint.title: ConstructionAxisInput.setByNormalToFaceAtPoint Method
setByNormalToFaceAtPoint.description: This input method if for creating a construction axis normal to a specified face or sketch profile and that passes through a specified point. This can result in a parametric or non-parametric construction axis depending on whether the parent component is parametric or is a direct edit component.
setByNormalToFaceAtPoint.return_description: Returns true if the creation of the ConstructionAxisInput is successful.

parameters.name: face
parameters.description: The face (BRepFace object) to create the axis normal to.

parameters.name: pointEntity
parameters.description: A construction point, sketch point or vertex the axis passes through. This point does not have to lie on the face.

related_samples.name: Construction Axis API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating construction axis in various ways.

setByPerpendicularAtPoint.title: ConstructionAxisInput.setByPerpendicularAtPoint Method
setByPerpendicularAtPoint.description: This input method is for creating an axis that is normal to a face at a specified point.
setByPerpendicularAtPoint.return_description: Returns true if the creation of the ConstructionAxisInput is successful.

parameters.name: face
parameters.description: A face (BRepFace object) to create the axis normal to.

parameters.name: pointEntity
parameters.description: A construction point, sketch point or vertex the axis is to pass through.

related_samples.name: Construction Axis API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating construction axis in various ways.

setByTwoPlanes.title: ConstructionAxisInput.setByTwoPlanes Method
setByTwoPlanes.description: This input method is for creating a construction axis coincident with the intersection of two planes or planar faces. This will fail if the two planes are parallel. This can result in a parametric or non-parametric construction axis depending on whether the parent component is parametric or is a direct edit component.
setByTwoPlanes.return_description: Returns true if the creation of the ConstructionAxisInput is successful.

parameters.name: planarEntityOne
parameters.description: The first planar face or construction plane to intersect

parameters.name: planarEntityTwo
parameters.description: The second planar face or construction plane to intersect

related_samples.name: Construction Axis API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating construction axis in various ways.

setByTwoPoints.description: This input method is for creating a construction axis that passes through the two points (work points, sketch points or vertices). This will fail if the two points are coincident. This can result in a parametric or non-parametric construction axis depending on whether the parent component is parametric or is a direct edit component.
setByTwoPoints.return_description: Returns true if the creation of the ConstructionAxisInput is successful.

parameters.name: pointEntityOne
parameters.description: The first construction point, sketch point or vertex the axis passes through

parameters.name: pointEntityTwo
parameters.description: The second construction point, sketch point or vertex the axis passes through

syntax.python:

propertyValue = constructionAxisInput_var.creationOccurrence

# Set the value of the property.
constructionAxisInput_var.creationOccurrence = propertyValue
